title: Takeda — Financials
html:
Takeda Financial Architecture

Governing Thought

Takeda is a financially under-documented account at ZoomRx — no AO Plan data, no FY25 base figure, no FY26 target, no pipeline value, and no profitability metrics are available from the SharePoint scan. What is visible is project volume: 11 active projects and 24+ historical projects, suggesting a multi-year engagement with meaningful cumulative revenue. The account's financial potential is disproportionately shaped by two pipeline events: the Entyvio biosimilar cliff (Takeda's ~$6-7B [UNVERIFIED] franchise facing first biosimilar 2025-2026) and the zasocitinib launch cycle (Phase 3 across 3 indications, potential launch 2027-2028). Both events create significant MR budget allocation opportunities. The immediate financial priority is populating baseline revenue data from Kimble and establishing an FY26 target to measure against.

Where to Find Financial Data

System What It Holds Access Update Cadence
Kimble Revenue, project-level financials, effort tracking, invoicing Internal ZoomRx (web app) Real-time as PMs update
Cluster Management (OneDrive) Revenue/Wins/Pipeline by cluster & account; utilization OneDrive, Base 1 file Weekly refresh
SharePoint (Takeda folder) Active project files, SOWs, proposals, call notes ZoomRx SharePoint Per project

Client MR Budget Intelligence

Takeda Corporate Financial Context

Metric Value Source Notes
FY2024 Revenue (ending Mar 2025) ~$28-29B [UNVERIFIED] Public filings (directional) Japan-based fiscal year ends March
R&D Spend ~15-17% of revenue [UNVERIFIED] Industry estimate Concentrated in GI, Rare Disease, Neuro
Entyvio Revenue ~$6-7B global [UNVERIFIED] Analyst estimates Largest brand; biosimilar cliff imminent
Cost Discipline Ongoing efficiency programs Public commentary Shire integration costs largely absorbed; zasocitinib investment phase

Budget Allocation Implications

Franchise-level dynamics that affect MR budgets:

  1. Entyvio (GI): As the largest brand, Entyvio likely has the largest MR budget within Takeda US. Biosimilar competition will either (a) increase MR spend to defend the brand, or (b) decrease MR spend as revenue erodes. The VERDICT prelaunch study suggests scenario (a) — Takeda is investing in franchise defense.

  2. Zasocitinib (Derm/Immunology): As a Phase 3 pipeline asset, zasocitinib's MR budget is currently pre-launch (smaller, focused on market shaping). Upon launch, this budget will expand significantly — launch-year MR budgets are typically 3-5x pre-launch budgets for major brands.

  3. Rare Disease (Takhzyro, Adzynma, Livtencity): Rare disease brands typically have smaller MR budgets but higher per-project willingness to pay (niche HCP audiences, complex methodologies). The HAE Medical Data Assessment is consistent with this profile.

  4. Neuroscience: Narcolepsy pipeline MR budgets are pre-commercial. Conference studies are the natural low-cost research format for pipeline assets at this stage.

  5. Hematology (Mezagitamab): Pre-commercial. Color Preference RT is a branding exercise consistent with early pre-launch investment.

Pricing Architecture

Observed Pricing Signals (from SharePoint Project Types)

Project Type Likely Price Range Takeda Projects Notes
PET $80-150K/wave Rare GI PET 2025-26, Gattex PET (historical) Depending on sample size, TA specialty, wave count
Conference Study $30-60K per conference [UNVERIFIED] Narcolepsy (3 conferences), AAN 2026 Pre/post design; lower cost than full tracking
Color Preference RT $25-50K [UNVERIFIED] Zasocitinib, Mezagitamab Niche methodology; quick turnaround
Radiographic RT $30-60K [UNVERIFIED] Psoriasis 2025 Specialist physician sample; includes image stimuli
Global Pulse $40-80K [UNVERIFIED] Zasocitinib Global Pulse (Nov 2025) Multi-market pulse survey
Medical Data Assessment $50-100K [UNVERIFIED] HAE (Oct 2025) Includes AE training materials, switching topline
Prelaunch Research $60-120K [UNVERIFIED] Entyvio VERDICT (Apr 2026) KO, Survey, Recruitment phases
AI Workshop $20-40K [UNVERIFIED] Oct 2025 One-time engagement; value is strategic, not revenue

Pricing note: All price ranges are estimates based on typical ZoomRx pricing for comparable project types. Actual Takeda pricing should be populated from SOWs on SharePoint.

Pricing Dynamics at Takeda

  • No confirmed preferred vendor status: Unknown whether ZoomRx has an MSA or preferred vendor agreement with Takeda. This affects pricing leverage and procurement cycle time.
  • Project-based SOWs: All visible projects appear to be standalone SOWs — no retainer or annual contract structure.
  • Japan-HQ procurement: For global brands (Entyvio, zasocitinib), procurement decisions may involve Japan-HQ sign-off, which can extend the sales cycle.

Revenue Context

ZoomRx Account Revenue

Metric Value Notes
FY25 Base [Populate from Kimble]
FY26 Target [Populate from Kimble or AO Plan]
FY26 YTD [Populate from Kimble]
Active Project Count 11 As of May 2026
Historical Project Count 24+ Since ~2019-2020
Profitability [Populate from Kimble] Expect healthy margins on niche methodology work

Revenue Estimation (Directional)

Based on 11 active projects and typical ZoomRx pricing:

Revenue Tier Estimate Assumption
Conservative $400-600K 11 projects at ~$40-55K average
Base case $600-900K Mix of PETs ($100K+), conference studies ($40-50K), and niche work ($25-50K)
Upside $900K-1.2M If Entyvio VERDICT, Rare GI PET, and zasocitinib work all convert to sustained tracking

Critical action: Populate actual revenue from Kimble. The estimation above is directional only.

Revenue Trajectory (Historical Trend)

Period Project Activity Revenue Signal
FY20-21 4-6 projects (Gattex PET, Motegrity PET, Ninlaro PET, Epilepsy PET, Vyvanse RT) Moderate — early relationship building
FY22-24 3-5 projects (Gattex PET renewals, ATU/PCA) Stable — GI tracking anchor
FY25 6+ projects (Rare GI PET, Conference Studies, Radiographic RT, HAE MDA, AI Workshop, zasocitinib Global Pulse) Accelerating — multi-TA expansion
FY26 YTD 4+ new projects (Entyvio VERDICT, zasocitinib Color Pref RT, mezagitamab Color Pref RT, AAN Conference) Continued acceleration

Pattern: Revenue has likely been growing year-over-year, with a step-change in H2 FY25 when the account expanded beyond GI into Rare Disease, Neuroscience, Derm/Immunology, and Hematology.

Revenue Growth Scenarios (FY26-FY28)

Scenario 1: Status Quo (No New Anchors)

If the account continues as a collection of one-off projects without establishing recurring trackers, revenue will fluctuate based on Takeda's project-by-project commissioning decisions. Expected range: $500-800K annually.

Scenario 2: One Anchor Tracker Established

If ZoomRx wins PET on one flagship brand (Entyvio biosimilar defense or zasocitinib launch), that single tracker adds $100-200K of predictable annual revenue. Combined with existing project work, the account reaches $800K-1.1M.

Scenario 3: Full Launch Suite Conversion

If zasocitinib launches and ZoomRx captures the full tracking suite (PET + ATU + Message Testing + Launch KPI + MSL across PsO and PsA), plus Entyvio competitive tracking, the account could reach $1.2-1.8M — rivaling Merck in scale.

Scenario FY27 Est. FY28 Est. Key Dependency
Status Quo $500-800K $500-800K Project pipeline continues at current pace
One Anchor $800K-1.1M $900K-1.2M Win PET on Entyvio or zasocitinib
Full Conversion $1.2-1.5M $1.5-1.8M Zasocitinib launch + Entyvio defense + MSL

Financial Questions to Answer

Revenue & Pipeline (Pull from Kimble)

  • What is the trailing-12-month revenue from Takeda? Breakout by project.
  • What is the current FY26 target? Who set it (AO Plan or default)?
  • What SOWs are signed but not yet invoiced?
  • What proposals are outstanding? Decision timelines?
  • What is the win rate on Takeda proposals over the last 2 years?

Profitability

  • What is the blended margin on Takeda work?
  • Are niche methodologies (Color Preference RT, Radiographic RT) higher-margin than PETs?
  • Is scope creep happening on any active project?

Pricing & Vendor Status

  • Does ZoomRx have a preferred vendor agreement or MSA with Takeda?
  • What is the standard procurement cycle time for Takeda US vs. Japan-HQ?
  • How does Takeda pricing compare to similar-sized accounts (Genmab, UCB)?

Cluster Context

  • Which cluster(s) execute Takeda work?
  • What share of that cluster's revenue comes from Takeda?
  • If zasocitinib launches and a full tracking suite is commissioned, does the executing cluster have capacity?

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
